product.name: ArWoWear T1 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 100% Cotton Twill, Flame Retardant Fabric, 220 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 2/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T1 Flame Pro is light weight 220 g/m², 100% cotton, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 certification.
It is mostly used as flame retardant lining.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
100% Cotton Twill, Flame Retardant Fabric, 220 gr/m'2
220 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 2/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>800N; Weft>600N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Flame retardant certification (EN ISO 11612) and FR lining use suggest possible protective clothing context, but no explicit firefighter/turnout gear use is stated.
FR workwear could theoretically be used by uniformed services, but no explicit military/police/tactical reference exists.
FR cotton fabric could be used in metalworking environments, but no explicit welding/foundry/metallurgy use is mentioned.

stage1.noMatchReason: Product is a generic flame-retardant cotton workwear/lining fabric certified to EN ISO 11612, explicitly described as used for flame retardant coveralls and jackets, without any explicit statement tying it to firefighting, military, welding, energy, or other listed application sectors.

evidence: categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
It is mostly used as flame retardant lining.
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly described as generic FR cotton workwear/lining used for coveralls and jackets, with no explicit tie to a narrower sector like firefighting, military, or welding. This matches workwear-industrial per rule 15.

secondary.rationale: No independent secondary application is explicitly supported beyond generic FR workwear use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Only generic FR certification and lining use; no explicit firefighter/turnout gear statement.
No explicit welding, foundry or metal-splash use stated.
